methods.description: This method describes the process of collecting geological samples from the field, performing geological mapping, preparing samples through polishing, and conducting initial mineralogical and micro-textural characterization using polarizing microscopy and SEM-EDS.

steps.description: Several field works were conducted from November 2019 to April 2023 in the Taebaeksan Basin of the Republic of Korea (South Korea).
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct field work and collect geological samples from the specified location.
inputs: Field site location (Taebaeksan Basin, South Korea)

steps.description: Based on the structural, sedimentological, and mineralogical characteristics of the traceable PBSC and carbonate rock unit, mapping was performed and a geological map was drawn (Supplementary Fig. 1).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform geological mapping based on sample characteristics and draw a geological map.

steps.description: All PBSC and carbonate rock samples were polished as slabs, thin sections, and epoxy mounts. Polishing was conducted in several steps, starting at 20 μm and finishing at 0.25 μm.
steps.searchable_summary: Polish geological samples to prepare slabs, thin sections, and epoxy mounts with specific finishes.
outputs: Polished samples (slabs, thin sections, epoxy mounts)

steps.description: Mineralogical and micro-textural characteristics were examined using a polarizing microscope...
steps.searchable_summary: Examine mineralogical and micro-textural characteristics using a polarizing microscope.

steps.description: ...and SEM-EDS with a back-scattered Electron (BSE) detector high-vacuum (HV) SEM (JSM-5610LV; JEOL Ltd., Tokyo, Japan) and an EDS system (Oxford Instruments, Abingdon, UK) at Yonsei University, Seoul, South Korea.
steps.searchable_summary: Examine mineralogical and micro-textural characteristics and elemental composition using SEM-EDS with BSE detector.

steps.description: In order to apply the in-situ method to isotope analysis, a route map was created to confirm the distribution and texture of the target mineral with merging compo images.
steps.searchable_summary: Create a route map for in-situ isotope analysis by merging SEM compo images to identify target mineral locations.

methods.method_name: In-situ Oxygen Isotope Analysis using SIMS
methods.description: This method describes the procedure for performing in-situ oxygen isotope analysis of quartz minerals using Large-Geometry Secondary Ion Mass Spectrometry (LG-SIMS), including sample preparation, instrument setup, signal acquisition, and use of a reference material.

steps.description: On the Au-coated epoxy mount surface with a thickness of 20 nm...
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare sample mounts for SIMS analysis by coating epoxy mounts with a 20 nm layer of gold.

steps.description: A total of 313 (including reference data) in-situ oxygen isotope analyses of quartz minerals were performed with LG-SIMS (CAMECA IMS1300-HR 3 Large-Geometry SIMS) from KBSI (Ochang, South Korea. ... with a 10 μm spot beam size. The signal was obtained in 20 cycles, and the measurement time was about 7-8 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Set up the LG-SIMS instrument for oxygen isotope analysis with specified parameters (spot size, cycles, measurement time).
inputs: Analysis parameters (10 μm spot, 20 cycles, ~7-8 min/analysis)

steps.description: ...δ 16 O quartz and δ 18 O quartz signals were obtained from the core to the rim of the grain with a 10 μm spot beam size. The signal was obtained in 20 cycles, and the measurement time was about 7-8 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire raw ion count signals for 16O and 18O isotopes from quartz grains using the configured LG-SIMS.
inputs: Route map (optional, for targeting specific areas)
outputs: Raw ion count data (16O, 18O signals) for sample spots

steps.description: As a reference material, Unil-Q 32 , natural crystals of quartz from Chile, provided by Lausanne University, was used.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire raw ion count signals for 16O and 18O isotopes from the reference material (Unil-Q) using the configured LG-SIMS.
outputs: Raw ion count data (16O, 18O signals) for reference material

steps.description: A total of 313 (including reference data) in-situ oxygen isotope analyses of quartz minerals were performed...
steps.searchable_summary: Process raw SIMS ion count data for samples and reference material to calculate oxygen isotope ratios (δ18O) using standard normalization procedures.
inputs: Known isotope value of reference material
outputs: Oxygen isotope ratios (δ18O) for sample spots
